_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Seven Ships, One Phone Call, Zero Leverage
context: Seven Malaysian tankers including Petronas and MISC vessels were stranded in the Strait of Hormuz after Iran blocked the waterway on 28 February 2026. PM Anwar secured passage through a direct call to Iran's president, but Malaysia was not on Iran's original safe-passage list.

cards.text: Seven Malaysian tankers cleared to pass Hormuz after PM Anwar's call to Iran's president.
cards.sub: Three Petronas, two MISC, one Sapura, one Sepadu vessel were awaiting clearance. Iran initially granted blanket passage only to China, Russia, India, Iraq and Pakistan. Malaysia was not on the list.

cards.h: Malaysia is a CMF member on paper but has no deployed naval assets in the Gulf, and was left off Iran's safe list.
cards.s: Japan runs an SDF mission near Hormuz without joining the US coalition. South Korea maintains a permanent Cheonghae Unit in the Gulf of Aden. Malaysia contributes nothing operational.
cards.d: Malaysia is a CMF member on paper but has no deployed naval assets in the Gulf, and was left off Iran's safe list. Japan runs an SDF mission near Hormuz without joining the US coalition. South Korea maintains a permanent Cheonghae Unit in the Gulf of Aden. Malaysia contributes nothing operational.

cards.h: Brent crude hit $126/barrel. The subsidy bill jumped to RM4 billion per month. Working households absorb the cost first.
cards.s: Higher crude boosts Petronas revenue. But the RM4B subsidy and rising costs land on B40 households, hawkers, fishers and rural commuters.
cards.d: Brent crude hit $126/barrel. The subsidy bill jumped to RM4 billion per month. Working households absorb the cost first. Higher crude boosts Petronas revenue. But the RM4B subsidy and rising costs land on B40 households, hawkers, fishers and rural commuters.

cards.h: Malaysia banned US warships while seeking Iran's goodwill: non-alignment that bought passage but no structural security.
cards.s: Non-alignment preserves diplomatic flexibility. But when ~2,000 ships are blocked globally and traffic drops 90%, flexibility without capability means waiting for a phone call.
cards.d: Malaysia banned US warships while seeking Iran's goodwill: non-alignment that bought passage but no structural security. Non-alignment preserves diplomatic flexibility. But when ~2,000 ships are blocked globally and traffic drops 90%, flexibility without capability means waiting for a phone call.

cards.text: The question is not whether the ships got through. It is whether a trade-dependent nation can keep relying on personal calls when the next strait closes.
Diplomacy works until it doesn't. Japan and South Korea built institutional Gulf presence without abandoning independent policy. Malaysia chose neither, and the gap showed.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
